heading: United States magistrate judge: appointment; functions; probation; fees
label: 460n–8 United States magistrate judge: appointment; functions; probation; fees
text: A United States magistrate judge shall be appointed for that portion of the Lake Mead National Recreation Area that is situated in Mohave County, Arizona. Such magistrate judge shall be appointed by the United States district court having jurisdiction thereover, and the magistrate judge shall serve as directed by such court, as well as pursuant to, and within the limits of, the authority of said court.The functions of the magistrate judge shall include the trial and sentencing of persons charged with the commission of misdemeanors and infractions as defined in . The exercise of additional functions by the magistrate judge shall be consistent with and be carried out in accordance with the authority, laws, and regulations, of general application to United States magistrate judges. The probation laws shall be applicable to persons tried by the magistrate judge and he shall have power to grant probation. The magistrate judge shall receive the fees, and none other, provided by law for like or similar services.section 3581 of title 18
notes: Amendments1988— struck out after second sentence of second par. “The provisions of title 18, section 3402, and the rules of procedure and practice prescribed by the Supreme Court pursuant thereto, shall apply to all cases handled by such magistrate.”Pub. L. 100–7021984—, substituted “magistrate” for “commissioner” wherever appearing in first par.Pub. L. 98–473, § 222(a), substituted provisions relating to trial and sentencing of persons charged with misdemeanors and infractions as defined in , for provisions relating to trial and sentencing of persons committing petty offenses as defined in title 18, section 1, and right of election of such persons to be tried in the district court of the United States.Pub. L. 98–473, § 222(b)section 3581 of title 18

Change of Name“United States magistrate judge”, “magistrate judge”, and “United States magistrate judges” substituted for “United States magistrate”, “magistrate”, and “United States magistrates”, respectively, wherever appearing in text pursuant to , set out as a note under , Judiciary and Judicial Procedure.section 321 of Pub. L. 101–650section 631 of Title 28
